.codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter4.stderr.txt:1091:.\docs\credibility_adaptive_weighting_and_bothsides_recs_2026_06_08.md:1:# POLARIS Source-Credibility & Multi-Perspective Recommendations Report\n### Driving two revisions to `docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md` — LAYER 1 (scoring) and LAYER 6 (composition + UI)\n\n**Author:** Lead analyst (Claude). **Date:** 2026-06-08.\n**Inputs:** three research streams (per-question adaptive credibility weighting; neutral multi-perspective presentation; friendly UI/artifact patterns).\n**Status:** revision driver. Each layer below states what it **REPLACES** in the Codex-APPROVE'd 2026-06-07 plan, then the concrete mechanism, auditability, reliability, and POLARIS wiring.\n\n**Invariants preserved across both revisions** (these are not negotiable and the revisions do not weaken them): the LLM credibility-judge is **advisory only** — `strict_verify`'s six per-sentence checks (`provenance_generator.py:1156`) remain the binding faithfulness gate; every new layer is **flag-gated, default-OFF byte-identical**, fail-loud on missing inputs; weight is **evidence quality/tier, never headcount**; every displayed aggregate is **one click from the cited `[#ev:id:start-end]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict**; the credibility prior stays **self-computed and verifier-overridable** (no single external rater hardwired); independence-collapse (`synthesis/independence_collapse.py`) still runs before any weighted tally; the contested-topic adjudication **reuses the existing 4-role / voter-arbiter pipeline** (`roles/role_pipeline.py` + `honest_sweep_integration.py`), open-weight voters only — no parallel path is built.\n\n---\n\n## 1. LAYER 1 (scoring): adaptive per-question credibility-skill, replacing the fixed domain-rubric selector\n\n### 1.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 2a/2b (L1)** selects one of **three fixed reliability rubrics** (clinical → RCT+peer-review+RoB; econ/policy → design-validity; qualitative → CERQual) keyed off the detected domain. That is still a small lookup table: domain → rubric. The operator constraint is explicit — **replace fixed domain rubrics** with a **generic, domain-agnostic credibility skill the LLM applies by reasoning per question**. Research stream #1's central finding is that the frontier (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude) has already moved this way, and that Perplexity's baked-in EEAT/domain-authority scoring is the closest-to-forbidden counter-example (gameable, popularity-biased, ~60% Google-overlap). We copy the reasoned-generic camp.\n\nThe revision: **domain stops being a selector and becomes context that primes one generic skill.** There is exactly one credibility skill; the detected `scope_template` domain is passed in as a hint the skill reasons over ("this is a clinical efficacy question, so weight trial design and RoB heavily"), not a branch that swaps rubrics. This scales to 10,000+ fields with zero per-field config.\n\n### 1.2 The exact mechanism — credibility-skill / guardrail prompt × (reliability × relevance)\n\n**Step A — deterministic signals (POLARIS produces these; unchanged substrate).** `authority_model.score_source_authority` and `tier_classifier` already emit, per source, the field-agnostic primitives: `authority_score [0,1]`, `source_class`, `corroboration_count` (independent-host agreement), `authority_confidence`, `signal_scores` (the A–E sub-scores), plus `junk_class` / `predatory_oa` (`source_class.py:71-89`). Independence-collapse (`independence_collapse.py`) emits `origin_cluster_id` + canonical-origin designation + copy flag. **None of this is replaced.** These are the inspectable, host-name-free, self-computed inputs (LAW VI: zero host literals in code).\n\n**Step B — the credibility skill consumes those signals and weights them per question.** A new pure prompt module, `authority/credibility_skill.py`, runs a **JudgeRank-style 3-step decomposition** (arXiv 2411.00142) — chosen because each step is inspectable text and is itself the audit template:\n\n1. **Query analysis** — identify what THIS question actually needs (the claim type, what would count as strong evidence for it).\n2. **Source analysis** — for each candidate source, a query-AWARE assessment applying **generic SIFT + lateral-reading + provenance heuristics**, NOT a domain table. The skill's instruction block encodes the domain-agnostic primacy heuristics that are OpenAI's own deep-research pattern: *prefer the primary/official source over an aggregator or SEO blog; prefer the original paper over a survey/secondary summary; corroborate laterally (does independent coverage agree); weigh source incentives/track record; downgrade stale-but-authoritative and retracted/superseded* (the latter wires to the existing `supersession.py` Phase 3 signal).\n3. **Two-axis judgment with rationale** — emit, per source, **RELIABILITY × RELEVANCE** (the EFSA/OHAT weight-of-evidence two-axis, which POLARIS currently conflates into one tier): reliability = internal validity/method given what the skill read in the signals; relevance = directness to this specific question. The product is the per-question credibility weight. **Crucially, the skill is told its priors are the `authority_model` signals and that it may up- or down-weight them with a stated reason** — it does not invent authority from nothing, and it cannot silently override `authority_confidence=LOW` into a fabricated HIGH (thin OpenAlex/ROR signal stays honestly LOW).\n\n**Output schema (machine-parseable, bound to the schema discipline already in §8.3.9):** per source — `{reliability_score, relevance_score, credibility_weight, rationale_string, signals_cited:[...]}`. The weight is **a visible, revisable rationale string, never a hidden scalar and never a fine-tuned-in behavior** (this is why we reject the CAG fine-tune approach from stream #1: opaque learned weights are exactly the auditability failure POLARIS differentiates against — borrow CAG's *evidence* that credibility-conditioning gives noise robustness, and its three generic dimensions of relevance/timeliness/reliability, but implement them at inference time as a prompt-visible signal).\n\n### 1.3 How it stays AUDITABLE (auditable by construction, not bolted on)\n\nThe judgment IS a reasoning trace, so it is emitted verbatim and **bound to the same provenance substrate POLARIS already has**:\n\n- The per-source `credibility_weight` + `rationale_string` + `signals_cited` attach to the source's `AuthorityResult` as **additive fields** (mirroring how `signal_scores`, `junk_class`, `predatory_oa` are already additive side-outputs on the dataclass), emitted on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n- At claim time, the weight flows into the four Phase-1/Phase-8 disclosure fields on `SentenceVerification` (`credibility_weight`, `independent_origin_count`, `certainty_label`, `span_verdict`), so each verified sentence shows **which source was weighted how, and why**, one click from its `[#ev]` span.\n- Because the skill cites the deterministic `signals_cited` it reasoned from, the rationale is **checkable against the inputs** — a reviewer can confirm "preferred A over B because A is the primary trial-registry entry and B a secondary news summary" against the actual `source_class` values. The rationale is a **claim to verify, not ground truth** (post-hoc rationalization risk per stream #1) — so it is advisory and the binding gate stays `strict_verify`.\n\nThis is the §-1.1-compliant story: no metadata/count/string-presence signal, every weight traceable to a cited span and an inspectable reason.\n\n### 1.4 Reliability and where it fails (and the guardrail)\n\nStream #1 is blunt about the failure modes, and they bound the design: LLMs "treat all retrieved documents as equally valid" unless explicitly steered and over-rely on high-frequency claims (arXiv 2505.17762); AuthorityBench (2603.25092) shows authority perception is **inconsistent across domains and highly prompt-sensitive**, struggles with false authority, and exhibits position/verbosity/self-preference bias. Therefore:\n\n- **The skill is ADVISORY to ranking/selection, never the faithfulness gate.** It changes *which rows are billed/weighted*, not *whether a sentence verifies its span*. `strict_verify`, 4-role/two-family segregation, and the independent `corpus_approval_gate` tier-distribution backstop all still run sequentially.\n- **Bias mitigations baked into the prompt:** score rationale-first then number; randomize candidate order to blunt position bias; run pointwise (per-source) to avoid listwise permutation instability.\n- **Offline validation before trust:** the chosen judge model+prompt is validated on an **AuthorityBench-style harness** (authority-PERCEPTION only, per the §6a no-overstatement correction — not as a volume-vulnerability benchmark) plus the existing vax adversarial fixture, before it is allowed on the shipping path. Calibration (Brier/ECE) on the emitted weights runs in the existing Phase 11 harness.\n- **Capture resistance:** multi-signal, self-computed, verifier-overridable — no NewsGuard/MBFC/Ad Fontes verdict is hardwired as ground truth (the FTC-capture risk).\n\n### 1.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L1\n\n**HAVE:** deterministic authority signals (`authority_model`, `tier_classifier`), the additive `AuthorityResult`/`SentenceVerification` schema, independence-collapse, `scope_templates` domain detection, the JSON-verdict schema discipline.\n**MISSING:** the generic per-question credibility skill itself, and the second (relevance) axis.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 2a/2b):**\n1. `authority/credibility_skill.py` — the pure 3-step (query→source→two-axis-judgment) prompt module emitting `{reliability, relevance, credibility_weight, rationale, signals_cited}`. Domain is an input *hint*, not a rubric switch. (Replaces the fixed-rubric selector in `credibility_prior.py`.)\n2. Add `relevance_score` (and the rationale fields) to `AuthorityResult` (`source_class.py:75`); emit on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n3. Wire the skill's weight into the L5 weight-mass tally and the L7 disclosure fields — advisory input, not a gate.\n4. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; OFF = the legacy single-scalar path, byte-for-byte.\n\n---\n\n## 2. LAYER 6 (composition + UI): neutral multi-perspective + transparent evidence-weight artifact, replacing the forewarning/fringe framing\n\n### 2.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 7 (L6)** composes contested claims as "consensus at high weight + minority attributed at low weight **with explicit forewarning**, medical-misinfo **low-weight/fringe**, ABSTAIN over fabricated balance." The operator directive is to **replace the forewarning/fringe framing** with a **neutral multi-perspective presentation where competing world-views are shown as legitimate, each carrying its real (possibly asymmetric) evidence weight, and the user judges** — no bias, no stamps, no forewarning prose.\n\n### 2.2 The hard tension, named and resolved (do not skip this)\n\nThere is a real conflict between two binding constraints, and both sides have a lethal failure mode:\n\n- The operator wants **no fringe/forewarning stamp, all sides legitimate, no bias.**\n- The **clinical-safety invariant #5** (binding, preserved) plus Dixon-Clarke 2013 (PubMed 23193194, measured harm: falsely-balanced vaccine reporting *reduced* perceived safety and vaccination intent), Wikipedia FRINGE, and §-1.1 all say **equal treatment of unequal evidence is itself bias** and is "lethal in clinical." I am not authorized to drop invariant #5.\n\n**The reconciliation — handed to us nearly verbatim by all three streams: replace the LABEL with the BASIS.** Wikipedia NPOV's "neutral *editing*, not neutral *content*"; GRADE's "show the basis, let the user infer"; the no-warning rule from stream #2 is satisfiable **if and only if the criteria behind any weight are displayed**. So:\n\n- The **system** stamps nothing — no "fringe," no "debunked," no forewarning sentence. That is the neutrality.\n- The **evidence weight** carries the (often asymmetric) truth, rendered as a visible **per-perspective evidence-basis panel** the user reads and judges. The asymmetry — *14 RCTs + 3 systematic reviews [High]* vs *2 case reports + mechanistic argument [Very Low]* — becomes **visible in the artifact** instead of **asserted in a stamp**. The user infers; the system does not editorialize.\n- "Legitimate" means **shown with its real (possibly low) weight and one-click drill-down to span + verdict** — NOT "shown as equally supported." A genuine world-view dispute with no empirical consensus (values, forecasting) shows steelmanned sides at honest parity; an empirical dispute with consensus shows the real weight distribution.\n- The medical-misinfo case is handled **without the banned word**: its panel is simply **near-empty of independent high-tier basis**, and where there is no independent consensus the claim **ABSTAINs** ("contested; no independent consensus" — reusin…795 tokens truncated…ntrasting / contextualizing) on each cited source — **neutral hue + icon + text label, never color alone** (colorblind safety; red=contrasting reads as red=wrong and biases toward consensus — explicitly avoided).\n4. **Drill-down:** every badge, every basis count, every panel is **one click from the raw `[#ev]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict** — the Scite "self-correcting" property: a mis-classified badge is exposed by the citing sentence sitting right behind it, and a meter without drill-down would violate the §-1.1 metadata-audit ban.\n\n**Panel sizing defeats false balance structurally, not with a caveat:** panels are **sized/annotated by real verified-evidence weight**, not equal-width — because equal real estate reads as equal credibility (the false-balance trap), and a caveat is empirically insufficient (Effectiviology / false-balance literature). The asymmetry is *in the layout and the basis panel*, not asserted in prose.\n\n**Three named anti-patterns explicitly rejected** (all from the streams, all as negative templates):\n- **Ground News source-count / coverage bar** — measures who-is-saying-this (volume), not evidence weight; §-1.1 bans source counts as quality signals. If a composition strip is shown at all, its axis is **evidence tier / source type**, never ideology or headcount, and it is kept visually separate from the weight panel so "many sources" never reads as "true."\n- **Bare confidence/credibility score (% or traffic-light dot)** — the best-documented HARM pattern: overconfident displays (80% shown / 70% accurate) pushed AI-advice adoption 57%→69.6%, 64% of users mis-judged calibration, and in a 2025 breast-cancer CDSS high confidence *reduced* diagnostic accuracy via overreliance. POLARIS's honest replacement is the per-sentence VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict against a real span — never a glanceable dot.\n- **Symmetric 50/50 equal-box presentation** — manufactures false balance; the Dixon-Clarke measured harm.\n\n### 2.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L6\n\n**HAVE:** the disclosure substrate (per-sentence `[#ev]` tokens, `strict_verify` verdict ladder, `SentenceVerification` additive fields), the existing 4-role/voter-arbiter agreement, independence-collapse for origin-count, and the full UI component kit (citation chip, verdict chip, Proof Replay, compare view).\n**MISSING:** the contestation-type classifier, per-perspective retrieval + composition that defeats false balance by weight, and the Perspectives artifact rendering basis panels.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 7):**\n1. A **contestation-type classifier** (empirical-consensus / genuine-uncertainty / values / forecasting) priming the composition rule — replaces the fixed forewarning+fringe rule.\n2. **Per-perspective retrieval + balance-rerank composition** over the post-collapse origins, weighting each perspective by verified-evidence quality × independent-origin count (not count).\n3. A **steelman + GRADE-style evidence-basis renderer** feeding a new **claim-anchored "Perspectives" panel** in the compare-view host: neutral headers, frequency-framed basis sub-panels, Scite-style neutral polarity badges, full span-verdict drill-down, weight-proportional sizing.\n4. Validated with a **paired-prompt neutrality eval** (Anthropic's open-sourced political-neutrality harness, adapted so it does NOT reward equal treatment of unequal evidence) + the vax adversarial fixture (asserts the asymmetry renders, no stamp, no 50/50).\n5. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; ABSTAIN reuses existing refuse/unresolved behavior; forewarning prose removed, replaced by the basis panel.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Consolidated "build" summary\n\n| Layer | Replaces (2026-06-07 plan) | POLARIS builds | Binding gate untouched |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **L1 scoring** | Fixed 3-rubric domain selector (Phase 2a/2b) | `credibility_skill.py` — generic 3-step SIFT/lateral-reading skill, reliability×relevance, rationale bound to `AuthorityResult`/`[#ev]`; domain as hint not switch; `relevance_score` axis | `strict_verify` binding; skill advisory; corpus_approval backstop |\n| **L6 composition+UI** | Forewarning + fringe framing (Phase 7) | Contestation-type classifier + per-perspective retrieval/balance-rerank weighted by quality×origin-count + steelman/GRADE-basis "Perspectives" panel (replace label with basis) | 4-role/voter-arbiter reused (no parallel path); ABSTAIN reuses refuse/unresolved; invariant #5 substance preserved |\n\nHonest residual: L1's skill is prompt-sensitive and must clear offline AuthorityBench-style + calibration validation before it is trusted on the shipping path; L6's steelman is the hardest element to audit mechanically (no test certifies an argument is the strongest version vs a disguised strawman — the paired-prompt eval is the best available check, itself a judgment). Both remain advisory; `strict_verify` carries faithfulness.\n\n---\n\n## 4. .codex\credibility-redesign\p4_diff_gate_verdict_iter4.stderr.txt:1216:.\docs\credibility_adaptive_weighting_and_bothsides_recs_2026_06_08.md:1:# POLARIS Source-Credibility & Multi-Perspective Recommendations Report\n### Driving two revisions to `docs/credibility_weighted_sourcing_redesign_plan_2026_06_07.md` — LAYER 1 (scoring) and LAYER 6 (composition + UI)\n\n**Author:** Lead analyst (Claude). **Date:** 2026-06-08.\n**Inputs:** three research streams (per-question adaptive credibility weighting; neutral multi-perspective presentation; friendly UI/artifact patterns).\n**Status:** revision driver. Each layer below states what it **REPLACES** in the Codex-APPROVE'd 2026-06-07 plan, then the concrete mechanism, auditability, reliability, and POLARIS wiring.\n\n**Invariants preserved across both revisions** (these are not negotiable and the revisions do not weaken them): the LLM credibility-judge is **advisory only** — `strict_verify`'s six per-sentence checks (`provenance_generator.py:1156`) remain the binding faithfulness gate; every new layer is **flag-gated, default-OFF byte-identical**, fail-loud on missing inputs; weight is **evidence quality/tier, never headcount**; every displayed aggregate is **one click from the cited `[#ev:id:start-end]` span and its VERIFIED/PARTIAL verdict**; the credibility prior stays **self-computed and verifier-overridable** (no single external rater hardwired); independence-collapse (`synthesis/independence_collapse.py`) still runs before any weighted tally; the contested-topic adjudication **reuses the existing 4-role / voter-arbiter pipeline** (`roles/role_pipeline.py` + `honest_sweep_integration.py`), open-weight voters only — no parallel path is built.\n\n---\n\n## 1. LAYER 1 (scoring): adaptive per-question credibility-skill, replacing the fixed domain-rubric selector\n\n### 1.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 2a/2b (L1)** selects one of **three fixed reliability rubrics** (clinical → RCT+peer-review+RoB; econ/policy → design-validity; qualitative → CERQual) keyed off the detected domain. That is still a small lookup table: domain → rubric. The operator constraint is explicit — **replace fixed domain rubrics** with a **generic, domain-agnostic credibility skill the LLM applies by reasoning per question**. Research stream #1's central finding is that the frontier (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude) has already moved this way, and that Perplexity's baked-in EEAT/domain-authority scoring is the closest-to-forbidden counter-example (gameable, popularity-biased, ~60% Google-overlap). We copy the reasoned-generic camp.\n\nThe revision: **domain stops being a selector and becomes context that primes one generic skill.** There is exactly one credibility skill; the detected `scope_template` domain is passed in as a hint the skill reasons over ("this is a clinical efficacy question, so weight trial design and RoB heavily"), not a branch that swaps rubrics. This scales to 10,000+ fields with zero per-field config.\n\n### 1.2 The exact mechanism — credibility-skill / guardrail prompt × (reliability × relevance)\n\n**Step A — deterministic signals (POLARIS produces these; unchanged substrate).** `authority_model.score_source_authority` and `tier_classifier` already emit, per source, the field-agnostic primitives: `authority_score [0,1]`, `source_class`, `corroboration_count` (independent-host agreement), `authority_confidence`, `signal_scores` (the A–E sub-scores), plus `junk_class` / `predatory_oa` (`source_class.py:71-89`). Independence-collapse (`independence_collapse.py`) emits `origin_cluster_id` + canonical-origin designation + copy flag. **None of this is replaced.** These are the inspectable, host-name-free, self-computed inputs (LAW VI: zero host literals in code).\n\n**Step B — the credibility skill consumes those signals and weights them per question.** A new pure prompt module, `authority/credibility_skill.py`, runs a **JudgeRank-style 3-step decomposition** (arXiv 2411.00142) — chosen because each step is inspectable text and is itself the audit template:\n\n1. **Query analysis** — identify what THIS question actually needs (the claim type, what would count as strong evidence for it).\n2. **Source analysis** — for each candidate source, a query-AWARE assessment applying **generic SIFT + lateral-reading + provenance heuristics**, NOT a domain table. The skill's instruction block encodes the domain-agnostic primacy heuristics that are OpenAI's own deep-research pattern: *prefer the primary/official source over an aggregator or SEO blog; prefer the original paper over a survey/secondary summary; corroborate laterally (does independent coverage agree); weigh source incentives/track record; downgrade stale-but-authoritative and retracted/superseded* (the latter wires to the existing `supersession.py` Phase 3 signal).\n3. **Two-axis judgment with rationale** — emit, per source, **RELIABILITY × RELEVANCE** (the EFSA/OHAT weight-of-evidence two-axis, which POLARIS currently conflates into one tier): reliability = internal validity/method given what the skill read in the signals; relevance = directness to this specific question. The product is the per-question credibility weight. **Crucially, the skill is told its priors are the `authority_model` signals and that it may up- or down-weight them with a stated reason** — it does not invent authority from nothing, and it cannot silently override `authority_confidence=LOW` into a fabricated HIGH (thin OpenAlex/ROR signal stays honestly LOW).\n\n**Output schema (machine-parseable, bound to the schema discipline already in §8.3.9):** per source — `{reliability_score, relevance_score, credibility_weight, rationale_string, signals_cited:[...]}`. The weight is **a visible, revisable rationale string, never a hidden scalar and never a fine-tuned-in behavior** (this is why we reject the CAG fine-tune approach from stream #1: opaque learned weights are exactly the auditability failure POLARIS differentiates against — borrow CAG's *evidence* that credibility-conditioning gives noise robustness, and its three generic dimensions of relevance/timeliness/reliability, but implement them at inference time as a prompt-visible signal).\n\n### 1.3 How it stays AUDITABLE (auditable by construction, not bolted on)\n\nThe judgment IS a reasoning trace, so it is emitted verbatim and **bound to the same provenance substrate POLARIS already has**:\n\n- The per-source `credibility_weight` + `rationale_string` + `signals_cited` attach to the source's `AuthorityResult` as **additive fields** (mirroring how `signal_scores`, `junk_class`, `predatory_oa` are already additive side-outputs on the dataclass), emitted on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n- At claim time, the weight flows into the four Phase-1/Phase-8 disclosure fields on `SentenceVerification` (`credibility_weight`, `independent_origin_count`, `certainty_label`, `span_verdict`), so each verified sentence shows **which source was weighted how, and why**, one click from its `[#ev]` span.\n- Because the skill cites the deterministic `signals_cited` it reasoned from, the rationale is **checkable against the inputs** — a reviewer can confirm "preferred A over B because A is the primary trial-registry entry and B a secondary news summary" against the actual `source_class` values. The rationale is a **claim to verify, not ground truth** (post-hoc rationalization risk per stream #1) — so it is advisory and the binding gate stays `strict_verify`.\n\nThis is the §-1.1-compliant story: no metadata/count/string-presence signal, every weight traceable to a cited span and an inspectable reason.\n\n### 1.4 Reliability and where it fails (and the guardrail)\n\nStream #1 is blunt about the failure modes, and they bound the design: LLMs "treat all retrieved documents as equally valid" unless explicitly steered and over-rely on high-frequency claims (arXiv 2505.17762); AuthorityBench (2603.25092) shows authority perception is **inconsistent across domains and highly prompt-sensitive**, struggles with false authority, and exhibits position/verbosity/self-preference bias. Therefore:\n\n- **The skill is ADVISORY to ranking/selection, never the faithfulness gate.** It changes *which rows are billed/weighted*, not *whether a sentence verifies its span*. `strict_verify`, 4-role/two-family segregation, and the independent `corpus_approval_gate` tier-distribution backstop all still run sequentially.\n- **Bias mitigations baked into the prompt:** score rationale-first then number; randomize candidate order to blunt position bias; run pointwise (per-source) to avoid listwise permutation instability.\n- **Offline validation before trust:** the chosen judge model+prompt is validated on an **AuthorityBench-style harness** (authority-PERCEPTION only, per the §6a no-overstatement correction — not as a volume-vulnerability benchmark) plus the existing vax adversarial fixture, before it is allowed on the shipping path. Calibration (Brier/ECE) on the emitted weights runs in the existing Phase 11 harness.\n- **Capture resistance:** multi-signal, self-computed, verifier-overridable — no NewsGuard/MBFC/Ad Fontes verdict is hardwired as ground truth (the FTC-capture risk).\n\n### 1.5 The honest gap + exactly what POLARIS builds for L1\n\n**HAVE:** deterministic authority signals (`authority_model`, `tier_classifier`), the additive `AuthorityResult`/`SentenceVerification` schema, independence-collapse, `scope_templates` domain detection, the JSON-verdict schema discipline.\n**MISSING:** the generic per-question credibility skill itself, and the second (relevance) axis.\n**BUILD (revision to Phase 2a/2b):**\n1. `authority/credibility_skill.py` — the pure 3-step (query→source→two-axis-judgment) prompt module emitting `{reliability, relevance, credibility_weight, rationale, signals_cited}`. Domain is an input *hint*, not a rubric switch. (Replaces the fixed-rubric selector in `credibility_prior.py`.)\n2. Add `relevance_score` (and the rationale fields) to `AuthorityResult` (`source_class.py:75`); emit on rows in `tier_classifier.py`.\n3. Wire the skill's weight into the L5 weight-mass tally and the L7 disclosure fields — advisory input, not a gate.\n4. Flag-gated default-OFF byte-identical; OFF = the legacy single-scalar path, byte-for-byte.\n\n---\n\n## 2. LAYER 6 (composition + UI): neutral multi-perspective + transparent evidence-weight artifact, replacing the forewarning/fringe framing\n\n### 2.1 What this REPLACES\n\nThe 2026-06-07 plan's **Phase 7 (L6)** composes contested claims as "consensus at high weight + minority attributed at low weight **with explicit forewarning**, medical-misinfo **low-weight/fringe**, ABSTAIN over fabricated balance." The operator directive is to **replace the forewarning/fringe framing** with a **neutral multi-perspective presentation where competing world-views are shown as legitimate, each carrying its real (possibly asymmetric) evidence weight, and the user judges** — no bias, no stamps, no forewarning prose.\n\n### 2.2 The hard tension, named and resolved (do not skip this)\n\nThere is a real conflict between two binding constraints, and both sides have a lethal failure mode:\n\n- The oper

async def generate_multi_section_report(
    *,
    research_question: str,
    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
    # I-meta-002-q1d (#948): campaign KG-reuse advisory context (prior-VERIFIED claims already
    # mechanically matched to THIS question's corpus). Passed through to the UNVERIFIED analyst layer
    # only; None/[] => no change. Never reaches the verified generator/strict_verify path.
    prior_verified_context: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
    # I-cred-012a (#1164): credibility-analysis pass inputs. Both None/empty => the pass is NOT run =>
    # byte-identical. Threaded by the sweep runner ONLY when PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN is on.
    credibility_pass_judge: Any = None,
    credibility_pass_gov_suffixes: tuple[str, ...] | None = None,
    model: Optional[str] = None,
    outline_temperature: float = 0.2,
    section_temperature: float = 0.3,
    outline_max_tokens: int = 2500,    # M-24 fix: was 800, JSON truncated with 12-20 ev_ids per section (V10 FATAL)
    section_max_tokens: int = 2400,    # M-24 fix: was 1200, bumped for 10-18 sentence target
    min_kept_fraction: float = 0.5,
    max_parallel_sections: int = 3,
    # R-1: pipeline telemetry for the Limitations synthesis call.
    tier_fractions: dict[str, float] | None = None,
    contradictions: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
    date_range: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
    limitations_temperature: float = 0.3,
    limitations_max_tokens: int = 400,
    # R-6 Gap-3: completeness-checklist uncovered topics surfaced to
    # the Limitations paragraph so the report acknowledges gaps.
    uncovered_topics: list[str] | None = None,
    # M-36 (2026-04-21): trial-summary table parameters. Enabled by
    # default; set `trial_summary_table_max_tokens=0` to disable.
    trial_summary_table_temperature: float = 0.2,
    trial_summary_table_max_tokens: int = 800,
    # M-42b (2026-04-22): named-trial anchors for deterministic
    # trial-table/timeline builder. When None/empty, LLM fallback
    # path runs (M-36 behavior).
    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None = None,
    # M-50 (2026-04-22): T2D-direct anchor set for per-trial
    # subsections. Only anchors in this set render subsections;
    # indirect (SURMOUNT-1/3/4) excluded. When None, defaults to
    # the full `primary_trial_anchors` set (caller responsibility to
    # filter). Empty set disables M-50.
    direct_trial_anchors: list[str] | None = None,
    # M-50 max tokens per subsection call
    m50_subsection_max_tokens: int = 400,
    m50_subsection_temperature: float = 0.2,
    # Codex M-63 REJECT Medium 2 fix: anchors whose primary trial
    # is already rendered by a V30 Phase-2 contract slot. M-50 MUST
    # skip these to avoid duplicating per-trial subsections — the
    # contract section owns the canonical "Trial X primary"
    # subsection via `render_slot_prose`. When None (default),
    # M-50 runs unchanged; sweep runner populates this from the
    # contract plans' entity_ids when `PG_V30_PHASE2_ENABLED=1`.
    m50_skip_anchors: set[str] | None = None,
    # M-52 (2026-04-23): V29-b. Full live_corpus (pre-selector
    # evidence_rows) so the generator can pull anchor-matched
    # primaries into evidence_pool when the selector missed them.

81:def run_credibility_analysis(
82:    research_question: str,
83:    rows: list[dict],
84:    *,
85:    gov_suffixes: tuple,
86:    domain: str | None = None,
87:    judge: Callable | None = None,
88:    now_year: int | None = None,
89:) -> CredibilityAnalysis:
90:    """Run the P4→P3→P2→P5→P6 chain over the EFFECTIVE evidence pool. Fail-loud on judge_error / missing eid.
91:
92:    ``rows`` MUST already be the generator's effective pool (post-M-52, post-dissent); ``gov_suffixes`` is
93:    the PSL gov-suffix tuple the rest of the pipeline uses (dependency-injected, no global). ``judge`` is the
94:    production credibility judge (injected); None ⇒ priors-only, which the runner forbids under activation.
95:    """
96:    if not rows:
97:        return CredibilityAnalysis({}, {}, [], [], [])
98:    if judge is None:
99:        # Codex I-cred-012 iter-5 P1: activation requires the PRODUCTION judge. P2 with judge=None returns
100:        # priors-only with judge_error=False, so a miswired master-on run would ship a false-green advisory.
101:        # The orchestrator is only ever called under activation, so a missing judge is fail-closed.
102:        raise CredibilityPassError(
103:            "abort_credibility_pass_error: the activated credibility pass requires a callable production "
104:            "judge; refusing to run priors-only (a false-green advisory). Wire the production judge or "
105:            "leave PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN off."
106:        )
107:    try:
108:        return _run_chain(
109:            research_question, rows,
110:            gov_suffixes=gov_suffixes, domain=domain, judge=judge, now_year=now_year,
111:        )
112:    except CredibilityPassError:
113:        raise
114:    except Exception as exc:  # ANY wired-module failure → fail-loud abort, never a silent false-green
115:        raise CredibilityPassError(
116:            f"abort_credibility_pass_error: a wired credibility module failed "
117:            f"({type(exc).__name__}): {exc}"
118:        ) from exc
119:
120:
121:def _run_chain(

130:    """The P4→P3→P2→P5→P6 chain body; wrapped by run_credibility_analysis for the fail-loud posture."""
131:    # ── P4: independent-origin collapse → per-row assignment, on COPIED rows (never mutate the caller) ──
132:    collapse = collapse_independent_origins(rows, gov_suffixes=gov_suffixes)
133:    if len(collapse.assignments) != len(rows):
134:        raise CredibilityPassError(
135:            "abort_independence_annotation_gap: P4 returned "
136:            f"{len(collapse.assignments)} assignments for {len(rows)} rows"
137:        )
138:    annotated: list[dict] = []
139:    origin_by_evidence: dict = {}
140:    for i, row in enumerate(rows):
141:        eid = _require_evidence_id(row, i)
142:        assignment = collapse.assignments[i]
143:        new_row = dict(row)  # COPY
144:        new_row["origin_cluster_id"] = assignment.origin_cluster_id
145:        new_row["is_canonical_origin"] = assignment.is_canonical_origin
146:        annotated.append(new_row)
147:        origin_by_evidence[eid] = assignment.origin_cluster_id
148:
149:    # ── P3: supersession multiplier per source ──
150:    supers_by_evidence = {
151:        _require_evidence_id(row, i): supersession_adjustment(row, now_year=now_year)
152:        for i, row in enumerate(annotated)
153:    }
154:
155:    # ── P2: credibility judgments — FAIL-LOUD on any judge_error under activation ──
156:    judgments = score_source_credibility(research_question, annotated, domain=domain, judge=judge)
157:    errored = [j.evidence_id for j in judgments if getattr(j, "judge_error", False)]
158:    if errored:
159:        raise CredibilityPassError(
160:            f"abort_credibility_pass_error: the production credibility judge failed for "
161:            f"{len(errored)} source(s) (e.g. {errored[:5]}); refusing to ship a priors-only false-green"
162:        )
163:
164:    # ── POST-P3 credibility = P2 weight × supersession multiplier (certainty carried, not folded away) ──
165:    credibility_by_evidence: dict = {}
166:    for row, judgment in zip(annotated, judgments):
167:        eid = judgment.evidence_id
168:        supersession = supers_by_evidence.get(eid)
169:        multiplier = supersession.multiplier if supersession else 1.0
170:        credibility_by_evidence[eid] = EvidenceCredibility(
171:            evidence_id=eid,
172:            credibility_weight=_clamp01(judgment.credibility_weight * multiplier),
173:            reliability_score=judgment.reliability_score,
174:            relevance_score=judgment.relevance_score,
175:            origin_cluster_id=origin_by_evidence.get(eid, ""),
176:            is_canonical_origin=bool(row.get("is_canonical_origin")),
177:            certainty_downgrade=bool(supersession.certainty_downgrade) if supersession else False,
178:            soft_warning=(supersession.soft_warning if supersession else None),
179:        )
180:
181:    # POST-P3 judgments for downstream — P6 disclosure must use the post-P3 credibility, not raw P2.

183:        dataclasses.replace(j, credibility_weight=credibility_by_evidence[j.evidence_id].credibility_weight)
184:        for j in judgments
185:    ]
186:
187:    # ── P5: claim graph (atomic claims + contradiction edges) ──
188:    graph = build_claim_graph(annotated, domain=domain)
189:
190:    # ── P6: origin-cluster weight-mass (mass = authority(canonical) ONLY; credibility disclosed) ──
191:    weight_mass = aggregate_weight_mass(graph.claims, annotated, adjusted_judgments)
192:
193:    return CredibilityAnalysis(
194:        credibility_by_evidence=credibility_by_evidence,
195:        origin_by_evidence=origin_by_evidence,
196:        claims=graph.claims,
197:        edges=graph.edges,
198:        weight_mass=weight_mass,

.codex\I-ready-012\codex_brief_verdict.stderr.txt:60:3. **PT08 evaluator input** — extend the evaluator/PT08 `contradictions` arg (`:4825`) to include `[asdict(r) for r in semantic_records]` so PT08 actively gates: a detected semantic contradiction whose subject+predicate is absent from report text → `pt08=False` → `abort_evaluator_critical`. Semantic records are NOT mixed into the numeric renderer loop `:4480-4494` (which expects numeric value ranges) — they render via their own block (point 2), so the numeric path is byte-identical.

 succeeded in 4490ms:
    if relevance is None:  # unknown / malformed relevance => multiplicative-neutral
        relevance = 1.0

    # Anti-fabrication cap: a LOW/thin source's reliability cannot exceed prior + max_uplift.
    if _is_low_or_thin(row):
        auth = _clamp01(row.get("authority_score")) or 0.0
        cap = _clamp01(auth + _float_env(_ENV_MAX_UPLIFT, _DEFAULT_MAX_UPLIFT))
        if cap is not None:
            reliability = min(reliability, cap)

    present = set(_present_signals(row))
    cited = [s for s in (raw.get("signals_cited") or []) if s in present]
    weight = _clamp01(reliability * relevance)
    return CredibilityJudgment(
        evidence_id=str(row.get("evidence_id", "")),
        reliability_score=reliability,
        relevance_score=relevance,
        credibility_weight=weight if weight is not None else 0.0,
        rationale=str(raw.get("rationale", "")),
        signals_cited=cited,
        query_need=str(raw.get("query_need", "")),
        judge_error=False,
    )


def score_source_credibility(
    research_question: str,
    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
    *,
    domain: str | None = None,
    judge: Optional[Callable[[str, dict], dict]] = None,
) -> list[CredibilityJudgment]:
    """Score each source on reliability × relevance — ADVISORY ONLY, pure, no row mutation.

    With ``judge=None`` returns one priors-only judgment per row (no network, total + offline). With
    an injected ``judge(research_question, payload) -> dict`` runs it PER SOURCE so a judge error is
    isolated to one row. ``domain`` is forwarded only as the ``domain_hint`` payload field — there is
    no domain-keyed branch or rubric table (operator: no fixed rubrics).
    """
    if judge is None:
        return [_priors_only_judgment(row) for row in (rows or [])]
    return [_apply_judge(research_question, row, domain, judge) for row in (rows or [])]

    if not pairs:
        return []
    if judge is None:
        judge = get_default_judge()
    return detect_semantic_conflicts(
        pairs, judge,
        min_confidence=_float_env(_ENV_MIN_CONFIDENCE, _DEFAULT_MIN_CONFIDENCE),
    )


# --- production judge (isolated; reuses the openrouter cost/family substrate) ---

_CONTRADICTION_PROMPT = """You are a strict cross-document contradiction judge. You are given two independent CLAIMS, each from a different source document, about a related subject. Decide their logical relation.

Rules:
- CONTRADICT: the two claims cannot both be true for the same population/endpoint — one asserts something the other explicitly denies or reverses (e.g. "improved overall survival" vs "no overall survival benefit"; "first-line therapy" vs "reserved for refractory cases").
- ENTAIL: the claims agree, or one restates / refines the other.
- NEUTRAL: the claims are about different things, or could both be true (different populations, endpoints, doses, or time points), or there is not enough overlap to judge a conflict.

Be conservative: only answer CONTRADICT when the disagreement is direct and on the same subject. Return STRICT JSON only, no prose:
{{"verdict": "CONTRADICT" | "ENTAIL" | "NEUTRAL", "confidence": <number 0.0-1.0>}}

CLAIM A:
{claim_a}

CLAIM B:
{claim_b}

JSON:"""


class _SemanticContradictionJudge:
    """Synchronous httpx wrapper around a cross-document contradiction call.

    Mirrors ``llm.entailment_judge._EntailmentJudge`` (same two-family evaluator
    model, the same ``openrouter_client`` cost/budget helpers, family segregation
    enforced at construction) but with a CONTRADICTION prompt and a (label,
    confidence) return. Kept SEPARATE from ``_EntailmentJudge`` so the
    faithfulness-critical strict_verify entailment path is never touched.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        import httpx

        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import check_family_segregation

        api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
        if not api_key:
            raise RuntimeError("PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
        self._api_key = api_key
        base_url = os.environ.get(
            "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
        ).rstrip("/")
        self._endpoint = f"{base_url}/chat/completions"
        self._model = os.environ.get("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL", _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL)
        # Two-family invariant (§9.1.1): the conflict judge is an evaluator-family
        # call and MUST differ from the generator family — raises at construction.
        check_family_segregation(evaluator_model=self._model)
        self._client = httpx.Client(timeout=_JUDGE_TIMEOUT_S)

    def judge(self, claim_a: str, claim_b: str) -> tuple:
        """Return ``(label, confidence)`` with label in {contradict,entail,neutral}.

        Fail-open on API/parse error → ("neutral", 0.0) so a transient outage never
        FABRICATES a conflict. ``BudgetExceededError`` is re-raised (the caller
        stops + keeps partial) — never masked as a neutral result.
        """
        from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc

        prompt = _CONTRADICTION_PROMPT.format(claim_a=claim_a, claim_b=claim_b)
        json_body: dict = {
            "model": self._model,
            "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
            "temperature": 0.0,
            "max_tokens": 60,
            "response_format": {"type": "json_object"},
        }
        try:
            from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb_for_routing
            _gate_provider = _pathb_for_routing.get_role_provider("evaluator")
        except Exception:
            _gate_provider = None
        if _gate_provider:
            json_body["provider"] = {
                "order": [_gate_provider],
                "allow_fallbacks": False,
                "require_parameters": True,
            }
        def _emit_raw_io(status: str, raw_response) -> None:
            # I-obs-001 #1141 AC3 (gate iter-1 P1): ONE raw-IO record per call tagged by its TRUE
            # outcome — "ok" only after a parsed verdict; "judge_error" on parse-failure / fail-open.
            try:
                _io_sink = _orc.current_raw_io_sink()
                if _io_sink is None:
                    return
                import uuid as _uuid
                _io_sink.record(
                    call_id=_uuid.uuid4().hex, call_type="nli_conflict_judge", role="evaluator",
                    request={**json_body, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]},
                    raw_response=raw_response, duration_ms=None, status=status,
                )
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
                pass

        data = None  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC3: bound before the try so the fail-open capture can prefer
        # the served response (post ok, parse failed) over the exception string.
        try:
            response = self._client.post(
                self._endpoint,
                headers={
                    "Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}",
                    "Content-Type": "application/json",
                },
                json=json_body,
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
            data = response.json()
            usage = data.get("usage", {}) or {}
            input_tokens = int(usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
            output_tokens = int(usage.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
            api_cost = float(usage.get("cost", 0) or 0)
            actual_cost = api_cost or _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
                self._model, input_tokens, output_tokens, 0,
            )
            if actual_cost == 0 and not usage:
                actual_cost = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(self._model, 400, 60, 0)
            _orc._add_run_cost(actual_cost)
            # FX-11b (#1117): also write the canonical cost-ledger ROW for this
            # NLI-conflict judge call. It already feeds the run BUDGET via
            # _add_run_cost above, but without a ledger row the persisted ledger
            # total trails the run-budget total whenever PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT is
            # on. Route through the same canonical writer + ambient-run-id key the
            # judge/role writers use, so all rows of one run share one accumulator.
            # append_cost_ledger_row bumps the SEPARATE ledger accumulator (not
            # _RUN_COST_CTX), so this does NOT double-count the run budget.
            # FX-11c (#1136): write the row BEFORE check_run_budget — a
            # budget-BREACHING call is already billed to the accumulator by
            # _add_run_cost, so it must also land in the ledger; if check_run_budget
            # raised first the breaching call would be billed-but-unledgered (the
            # exact ledger<budget drift FX-11/FX-11b exist to eliminate).
            try:
                _orc.append_cost_ledger_row(
                    session_id=_orc.current_run_id() or "",
                    call_type="nli_conflict_judge",
                    cost_usd=actual_cost,
                    input_tokens=input_tokens,
                    output_tokens=output_tokens,
                )
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — ledger I/O must never abort detection
                pass
            _orc.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if cap breached
            content = data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
            parsed = json.loads(content)
            verdict = str(parsed.get("verdict", "")).strip().upper()
            confidence = float(parsed.get("confidence", 0.0) or 0.0)
            label = {"CONTRADICT": "contradict", "ENTAIL": "entail",
                     "NEUTRAL": "neutral"}.get(verdict, "neutral")
            # Parsed verdict → the ONE success raw-IO record, AFTER parse (gate iter-1 P1).
            _emit_raw_io("ok", data)
            return label, confidence
        except Exception:
            # BudgetExceededError is a subclass-free RuntimeError in openrouter_client;
            # re-raise it explicitly so the caller's keep-partial path fires.
            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import BudgetExceededError
            import sys
            exc = sys.exc_info()[1]
            if isinstance(exc, BudgetExceededError):
                raise
            logger.warning("[semantic-conflict] judge call failed (fail-open neutral): %s", exc)
            # I-obs-001 #1141 AC3: capture the fail-OPEN judge_error — like the entailment judge, this
            # silently drops a possible real conflict on a transient/parse failure (drb_72-class
            # signal). Prefer the bound served `data`. Default-OFF; never raises.
            try:
                _io_sink = _orc.current_raw_io_sink()
                if _io_sink is not None:
                    import uuid as _uuid
                    _io_sink.record(
                        call_id=_uuid.uuid4().hex, call_type="nli_conflict_judge", role="evaluator",
                        request={**json_body, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]},
                        raw_response=(data if data is not None else {"error": str(exc)}),
                        duration_ms=None, status="judge_error",
                    )
            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
                pass
            return "neutral", 0.0


_JUDGE_SINGLETON = None


def get_default_judge():
    """Lazy singleton production judge callable ``(a, b) -> (label, confidence)``.

    Constructed only when ``PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT`` is ON and first used — off-mode
    never instantiates it (no network, no httpx client)."""
    global _JUDGE_SINGLETON
    if _JUDGE_SINGLETON is None:
        _JUDGE_SINGLETON = _SemanticContradictionJudge()
    return _JUDGE_SINGLETON.judge

This is the IMPLEMENTATION-APPROACH brief for the FIRST and DEEPEST activation hook — running the
committed `synthesis/credibility_pass.run_credibility_analysis` orchestrator in the LIVE path over the
generator's EFFECTIVE evidence pool, under the locked I-cred-012 architecture (force-APPROVE iter-5).
NO code yet — confirm the hook placement + threading + fail-closed against the spend-bearing generation
core BEFORE I edit it. The per-resolve-site P8 render is 008b; the both-sides appendix is 007b; this
issue is ONLY: run the pass over the effective pool + thread its outputs to where 008b/007b consume them.

## 0. HARD CONSTRAINTS

- ADVISORY only — strict_verify + 4-role D8 stay the ONLY binding gates. default-OFF `PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN` ⇒ byte-identical (no call, no thread, no signature behavior change).
- FAIL-LOUD (drb_72 lesson): the orchestrator already aborts on missing eid / judge_error / judge-absent. The runner must pass the PRODUCTION judge + gov_suffixes; under the master slate the fail-closed preflight asserts they're present.
- Do NOT mutate the binding flow: generation, strict_verify, D8 unchanged when OFF.

## 1. Grounded sites (already read)

- Runner generation call: `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:4707` `multi = await generate_multi_section_report(research_question=q["question"], evidence=evidence_for_gen, ...)`.
- Generator M-52 effective-pool pull: `multi_section_generator.py:4628` `_m52_pull_from_live_corpus(evidence_pool, live_corpus, primary_trial_anchors)`; M-44 injection 4638-4669. After ~4670 `evidence_pool` is the EFFECTIVE pool (every row the generator can cite).
- P8 consumes the analysis at the 4 resolve sites (2264 / 4910+4950 alias / contract 845 / quantified 380) — 008b.

## 2. The implementation decision (PLEASE CONFIRM)

The architecture P1-2 said: run the pass over the EFFECTIVE pool — "hoist the M-52 pull before the pass, OR define the universe as the generator's effective evidence pool." Two options:

- **(A) Generator-internal pass:** call `run_credibility_analysis` INSIDE `generate_multi_section_report`, just AFTER the M-52/M-44 block (~4670), over the post-M-52 `evidence_pool`. The generator gets `credibility_judge` + `gov_suffixes` + the master flag as new optional params; the resulting `CredibilityAnalysis` is held in the generator and handed to the per-section P8 wrapper (008b). PRO: sees the true effective pool with no refactor. CON: the generator now imports `credibility_pass` + carries the judge.
- **(B) Runner hoist:** lift the M-52 selection out of the generator to the runner, run the pass in the runner over the hoisted effective pool, pass `CredibilityAnalysis` into `generate_multi_section_report` as a param. PRO: keeps the generator pure-ish. CON: refactors M-52 (a belt-and-suspenders safety net) — higher regression risk in the spend-bearing path.

**I lean (A)** — least disturbance to the M-52 safety net, the pass sees exactly what the generator cites, and the flag-gate keeps OFF byte-identical. **Q1: confirm (A) vs (B)**, given (A) makes the generator depend on the credibility chain.

**Q2 — flag-gate + signature:** new params default to None/off so OFF is byte-identical (no pass, analysis=None, downstream P8 no-ops). Confirm the param-default approach can't change any existing call's behavior.

**Q3 — fail-closed preflight:** under master-on, assert `credibility_judge` callable + `gov_suffixes` non-empty BEFORE generation, else abort (not a silent skip). Confirm placement (runner preflight vs first generator use).

**Q4 — dissent/M-52 ordering:** 010b's append-only dissent round runs in the runner BEFORE generation and re-certs plan_sufficiency; so by the time generation's M-52 runs, `evidence_for_gen` already includes dissent rows. Confirm (A) still sees them (it runs after M-52 over the merged pool).

**Q5 — telemetry/no-throttle:** the pass must not silently drop rows from `evidence_for_gen` (no capability downgrade). It is READ-ONLY over the pool (annotates copies); confirm it cannot shrink the generator's citeable set.
